mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over the preceding 3 months signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.66% as of 2017-02-01 indicates accommodative policy stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines do not exceed baseline returns by a statistically significant margin (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals labor market deterioration that triggers equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7% as of 2017-02-01 indicates tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite unemployment rise, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window during trigger-active periods.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) over the preceding 2 months signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism requires simultaneous weakness in both series to activate.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 473991M and HOUST at 1246K as of 2017-01-01
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term (RSAFS × HOUST) produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above its 12-month trend, signaling supply-side expansion that supports corporate profit margins and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously as a trend-following signal.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during INDPRO acceleration periods do not exceed baseline by a statistically significant margin (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100K new jobs per month over a 3-month rolling average, signaling labor market slack that reduces consumer spending power and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when this threshold is crossed.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth falls below 100K jobs/month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window during periods when PAYEMS growth is below threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 1.5%, signaling monetary policy lag that creates expectations of future rate hikes, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors reprice duration risk.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 244.456 and FEDFUNDS at 0.66% as of 2017-02-01
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with rising inflation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term (CPIAUCSL × FEDFUNDS) produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 1.5% annualized rate in the most recent quarter, signaling economic slowdown that reduces equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following the GDP release.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window during periods of sub-1.5% GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above their 12-month trend while retail sales (RSAFS) remain stable or growing, signaling robust consumer demand and housing investment that supports corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism requires HOUST acceleration without RSAFS contraction.
candidate_trigger: HOUST growth > 12-month trend AND RSAFS does not decline > 1% MoM
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1246K and RSAFS at 473991M as of 2017-01-01
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 45% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term (HOUST × RSAFS) produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
